options.content:
# **🚀 kube-burner-ocp virt ephemeral restart benchmark dashboard**

This dashboard provides deep insights into the results of the virt-ephemeral-restart benchmark execution using kube-burner-ocp. For a deep dive into workload parameters and metric definitions, refer to the [official kube-burner-ocp documentation](https://kube-burner.github.io/kube-burner-ocp/latest/#virt-ephemeral-restart).

### ⚙️ How to configure your view

Use the dropdown selectors at the top of the dashboard to filter the results:

- **Run UUID 🔽:** Select the unique identifier for your benchmark execution to filter all visualizations for that specific test run.
- **Latency Percentile 📊:** Toggle between median (50th) for typical performance or tail latencies (90th/95th/99th) to identify worst-case bottlenecks in your infrastructure.

📌 **NOTE:**
- All latency metrics in this dashboard are reported in seconds.
- Since the OS image is pulled directly into the `virt-launcher` pod’s local directory (typically on the node’s root disk), no DataVolumes or PVCs are created or bound for this scenario.

targets.query: jobConfig.name.keyword: delete-ephemeral-volumes-* AND metricName.keyword:"jobSummary" AND uuid:"$uuid"
jobConfig.name.keyword: start-ephemeral-vms-* AND metricName.keyword:"jobSummary" AND uuid:"$uuid"

panels.title: Workload preparation - Create base image DV

list.description: Filters the dashboard to show performance metrics for the selected virt-ephemeral-restart benchmark run ID
list.label: Run UUID
list.name: uuid

list.description: Selects the P-value (50th to 99th) for VM and DV latency calculations
list.label: Latency Percentile
list.name: percentile
